topic: Someone receives a package intended for the previous tenant
raw_output:
[story 1]

The doorbell rang, a jarring intrusion into the dull silence of my afternoon. I squinted through the peephole, my jaw tightening before I even moved. A package sat by the mat, a cardboard box addressed to the previous occupant, a fact that immediately soured my mood. I sighed, a long, drawn-out sound that seemed to vibrate the stale air of the hallway. With a heavy groan, I retrieved the box. It was bulky and smelled faintly of old paper and something vaguely floral. I dragged it across the worn linoleum floor into my cramped living room, eyeing it with profound distaste. "Great," I muttered to the empty room, shoving the box onto the nearest surface as if it were a personal affront. I really needed a nap, and now I had to deal with unsolicited deliveries.

[story 2]

I was wrestling with a particularly stubborn jar lid when the delivery notification pinged. Another one. I didn't even bother looking up immediately; my shoulders were already hunched, a defensive posture against the world. When I finally turned, the package sat there, a glaring splash of unwanted mail. My eyes narrowed, taking in the generic shipping label. *Of course,* I thought, a sharp, internal retort forming instantly. I didn't want anything from the past, or anyone. With deliberate slowness, I picked up the box, my fingers brushing against the rough cardboard as if it were contaminated. I carried it to the kitchen counter, placing it down with a decisive thud that echoed my general state of agitation. It just needed to be dealt with, quickly and without further interaction.

[story 3]

The sound of the delivery truck idling outside grated on my nerves. I was trying to focus on sorting through a stack of overdue bills, but the insistent chime of the intercom pulled my attention away. When I opened the door, I saw it—a package clearly meant for someone else. A wave of immediate irritation washed over me, making my brow furrow deeply. "Seriously?" I snapped, the word sharp and brittle. I snatched the parcel from the doormat, my movements jerky and impatient. I didn't want to acknowledge the sender, didn't want to engage in any unnecessary exchange. Shoving the box into the corner of the room, I leaned against the wall, crossing my arms tightly over my chest, radiating an absolute refusal to be disturbed.
